footnote-md: This property should be false, since the increment of the `donation` map could overflow.
This property should always be false, since a contract can receive ETH when its address is specified in a coinbase transaction or in a `selfdestruct`.
This property is false since donors can reclaim their donations (after the donation period has ended and the goal has not been reached).
All funds may have been reclaimed already.
Receiver of the funds may revert the transaction.

This property should always be false, since during the external call in `withdraw`, ether can be forcibly sent to Crowdfund. This leaves a positive balance in the Crowdfund contract after `withdraw` executes.
This property is false, since `owner` can be a contract which can redirect the received funds to another address.
This property should always be false, since `donate` does not require `msg.value > 0`. Hence, if a user donates with a `msg.value == 0`, then the `donation` map of no user changes.
This property is false since since the `msg.sender` can be a contract that redirects the funds received from Crowdfund to another address.
